steps.title: Calculate calcium from milk
steps.content:
Each cup of milk has 304 mg of calcium. The batch uses 4 cups, so the total calcium from milk is:
$$4 \times 304 = 1216 \text{ mg}$$

steps.title: Find calcium from bananas
steps.content:
The batch contains 1,228 mg total. The calcium from bananas is:
$$1228 - 1216 = 12 \text{ mg}$$
This is from 2 bananas combined.

steps.title: Divide by number of bananas
steps.content:
Dividing by the number of bananas:
$$\frac{12}{2} = 6 \text{ mg per banana}$$
That matches Choice D — 6.
